data.title: Iranian Revolutionary Guard Attacks US Bases in UAE and Kuwait
data.description: Iranian state media reports that the Iranian Revolutionary Guard has launched attacks on the US military stationed at the Al Dhafra Air Base in the United Arab Emirates and the Ali Al Salem Air Base in Kuwait. According to Jin10, these actions mark a significant escalation in regional tensions, potentially impacting geopolitical stability in the Middle East. The attacks come amid ongoing disputes and heightened military activities in the area, raising concerns about further conflict and its implications for international relations.

data.title: A trader opened a short position in WTI crude oil with 3x leverage, at a liquidation price of $114.88.
data.description: According to Lookonchain monitoring, WTI crude oil futures prices surged, but trader "0x4ff9" bet against the trend by opening a 3x leveraged short position in WTI crude oil with a liquidation price of $114.88. The current position holds 30,000 shares of WTI crude oil futures.

data.title: U.S. December Business Inventories Show Slight Increase
data.description: The U.S. December business inventories rose by 0.1%, aligning with expectations, according to Jin10. The previous value was revised from 0.10% to 0%, indicating a minor adjustment in the data. This slight increase in inventories suggests a stable economic environment, with businesses maintaining consistent stock levels. The revision of the prior month's data reflects ongoing adjustments in economic reporting, highlighting the importance of accurate inventory tracking for economic analysis.

data.title: Russian Urals Oil Delivery Price Surpasses Benchmark at Indian Ports
data.description: Russian Urals crude oil has seen its delivery price at Indian ports exceed the benchmark for the first time, according to Jin10. This development marks a significant shift in the pricing dynamics of Russian oil exports to India, reflecting changes in market conditions and demand. The price increase could have implications for global oil markets, as India is a major importer of Russian crude. Traders are closely monitoring the situation to assess its potential impact on future trade and pricing strategies.

data.title: Kevin Hassett Acknowledges Utility of Prediction Markets
data.description: White House National Economic Council Director Kevin Hassett has expressed that prediction markets hold value in certain contexts. According to NS3.AI, Hassett's remarks highlight the potential applications of prediction markets in economic forecasting and decision-making processes. While he did not elaborate on specific uses, his acknowledgment suggests a recognition of their role in providing insights into market trends and public sentiment. Prediction markets, which allow participants to bet on the outcomes of future events, have been gaining attention for their ability to aggregate diverse opinions and information. Hassett's comments may indicate a growing interest in exploring these markets as tools for economic analysis and policy development.

data.title: BlackRock Limits Withdrawals Amid Rising Redemption Requests
data.description: BlackRock has restricted withdrawals from one of its largest private credit funds following a surge in client redemption requests. Bloomberg posted on X, highlighting growing concerns among retail investors about the $1.8 trillion private credit sector. This move reflects increasing anxiety within the industry as investors seek to pull back their investments. The decision underscores the challenges faced by private credit funds in managing liquidity amid heightened demand for redemptions.

data.title: Trump's Iran Stance Impacts Markets, Bitcoin and Stocks Decline
data.description:
U.S. President Donald Trump declared that there will be "no deal with Iran except UNCONDITIONAL SURRENDER," which has influenced market movements. According to NS3.AI, this statement pushed WTI crude oil prices close to $90 per barrel, while causing declines in both bitcoin and stock markets. Nasdaq futures experienced a 1.8% drop, and bitcoin fell by 5% to approximately $68,800 as risk markets reacted negatively.

In addition to these developments, separate data revealed a decrease in U.S. payrolls by 92,000 jobs in February, with the unemployment rate rising to 4.4%. Despite the weak labor market data, interest rate markets are currently pricing in only a 4% probability of a Federal Reserve rate cut in March and a 17% chance of a cut in April.

data.title: U.S. Secretary of State Rubio Accuses Rwandan Officials of Fueling Instability in Eastern Congo
data.description: U.S. Secretary of State Marco Rubio has accused Rwandan officials of exacerbating the unrest in the eastern Democratic Republic of the Congo (DRC). According to Jin10, Rubio's statement highlights the ongoing tensions in the region, where various armed groups have been active. The eastern DRC has long been plagued by conflict, with neighboring countries often being implicated in the turmoil. Rubio's remarks underscore the international concern over the situation and the role of regional actors in either mitigating or aggravating the conflict. The U.S. continues to monitor the developments closely, emphasizing the need for diplomatic efforts to restore stability in the area.

data.title: In the past hour, $112 million in liquidations occurred across the entire network, with $55.19 million in BTC liquidations.
data.description: According to Coinglass data, $112 million in positions were liquidated across the network in the past hour, including $109 million in long positions and $3.76 million in short positions. In addition, $55.19 million in BTC positions were liquidated and $28.12 million in ETH positions were liquidated.

data.title: Institutions: Lower-than-expected non-farm payrolls provide talking points for the Fed's dovish stance, but recent factors may weaken the necessity for rate cuts.
data.description: David Rees, Global Head of Economics at Schroders, stated that the significantly weaker-than-expected non-farm payroll data will provide ample material for discussion among doves within the Federal Reserve. However, at least part of the disappointing figure was due to strikes in the healthcare sector, a situation that should be reversed. Furthermore, despite the weak jobs report, we believe that continued growth in labor demand is inevitable given the ongoing strong growth of the US economy. It remains unclear whether incoming Fed Chairman Kevin Warsh will change his view that the application of artificial intelligence will significantly improve US productivity and create room for lower interest rates. However, any recovery in the job market, and the inflationary risks posed by events in the Middle East, will diminish the need for a rate cut in the short term. (Jinshi)

data.title: White House Monitors Private Credit Market
data.description: The White House is closely monitoring the private credit market, according to Jin10. Kevin Hassett, Director of the National Economic Council, emphasized the administration's focus on this sector. The attention comes amid concerns about potential risks and the overall health of the financial system. The private credit market has been under scrutiny due to its rapid growth and the increasing involvement of non-bank lenders. The administration aims to ensure stability and mitigate any adverse impacts on the broader economy.

data.title: Colombian Presidential Candidate Warns Against Populist Influence
data.description: Presidential candidate Roy Barreras has expressed concerns about Colombia's vulnerability to populist leaders advocating radical and extreme policies. Bloomberg posted on X, highlighting Barreras' warning that such political figures could sway the nation with their rhetoric. Barreras emphasized the importance of maintaining stability and avoiding drastic shifts that could impact Colombia's future. His comments come amid a broader discussion on the political landscape in Colombia, where various candidates are vying for influence ahead of the upcoming elections. Barreras' remarks underscore the challenges faced by the country as it navigates potential changes in leadership and policy direction.

data.title: White House Economic Advisor Comments on Tariff Rebate Lawsuits
data.description: White House National Economic Council Director Kevin Hassett has expressed attention to lawsuits initiated by large corporations concerning tariff rebates. According to Jin10, Hassett's remarks highlight the ongoing legal challenges faced by businesses in response to tariff policies. The focus on these lawsuits underscores the broader economic implications and the potential impact on corporate strategies. The developments come amid a backdrop of trade tensions and economic policy adjustments.

data.title: U.S. Labor Market Faces Challenges After February Employment Report
data.description: Federal Reserve Bank of San Francisco President Mary Daly expressed concerns over the February employment report, which she described as disappointing. Bloomberg posted on X, highlighting Daly's remarks that the data challenges the idea of a stabilizing U.S. labor market. Daly's comments come amid ongoing discussions about the economic recovery and the Federal Reserve's monetary policy strategies. The employment figures have raised questions about the pace of recovery and the potential need for continued support from the central bank. Daly emphasized the importance of closely monitoring labor market trends to inform future policy decisions.

data.title: U.S. President Trump Predicts Imminent Collapse of Cuba Amid Conflict
data.description: U.S. President Donald Trump has expressed strong opinions regarding the ongoing conflict, suggesting that if rated on a scale of ten, the situation could reach a severity level of twelve or fifteen. According to Jin10, Trump also stated that Cuba is on the brink of collapse, indicating a potential shift in geopolitical dynamics. The remarks highlight the escalating tensions and the possible implications for international relations.

data.title: Bloomberg analysts: The 21Shares Polkadot ETF has an expense ratio of 0.3% and initial seed funding of approximately $11 million.
data.description: Eric Balchunas, a senior ETF analyst at Bloomberg, wrote on the X platform that, according to disclosed information, 21Shares launched its first spot Polkadot ETF in the US market today with a fee rate of 0.30% and initial seed funding of approximately $11 million. In its product description, 21Shares stated that Polkadot's unique feature is its design to connect multiple independent blockchains into an interoperable network, enabling developers to build and run their own custom blockchains on top of it.

data.title: Economist Peter Schiff Warns of Potential Market Impact from US-Iran Conflict
data.description:
Economist Peter Schiff has issued a warning regarding the potential market impact if the conflict between the United States and Iran escalates and prolongs. According to NS3.AI, Schiff suggests that stocks, bonds, cryptocurrencies, and the US dollar could experience declines, while gold and oil prices might surge. Schiff believes that current market pricing assumes a brief and successful conflict, a scenario he deems unlikely.

In a separate analysis, Rashad Hajiyev, an analyst, forecasts that gold prices could rise significantly, reaching between $7,000 and $8,000 in the future. Hajiyev attributes this potential increase to the beginning of a new advancing cycle in precious metals and mining stocks.

data.title: Analysts: The Federal Reserve is caught in a dilemma due to the latest jobs report deviating from expectations.
data.description: Brian Jacobsen, chief economist at Annex Wealth Management, said the non-farm payroll data was disappointing and deviated significantly from expectations. The report's data cannot be embellished; the rising unemployment rate, coupled with a sharp increase in oil prices, will raise concerns among traders about the risk of stagflation. The Federal Reserve will face a dilemma: should it take measures to help the job market, or maintain the status quo (or even threaten to raise interest rates) to curb inflation expectations? (Jinshi)

data.title: U.S. Military Plans to Ensure Safe Passage for Ships
data.description: The White House National Economic Council Director, Kevin Hassett, announced that the U.S. military is formulating a plan to ensure the safe passage of ships. According to Jin10, this development comes amid rising geopolitical tensions that could potentially impact global trade routes. The initiative aims to safeguard maritime navigation and maintain stability in international waters. Details of the plan have not been disclosed, but it underscores the U.S. commitment to protecting its interests and allies in key regions. The move is part of broader efforts to address security challenges and ensure the free flow of commerce across the seas.

data.title: U.S. White House Economic Adviser Comments on Iran and Oil Issues
data.description: U.S. White House National Economic Council Director Kevin Hassett stated that there will be no immediate discussions on utilizing the Strategic Petroleum Reserve (SPR) in relation to Iran and oil issues. According to Jin10, this announcement comes amid ongoing geopolitical tensions and fluctuating oil markets. The decision reflects the current administration's stance on managing national energy resources without resorting to emergency reserves. This approach aims to maintain stability in the energy sector while addressing international concerns.
